Paragraph.Text: has sent to the Director a document required to be sent under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all required fees; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: exists as of a certain date.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Director may refuse to issue certificate of existence
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the Director may refuse to issue a certificate described in paragraph (1)(c) if the Director has knowledge that the corporation is in default of sending a document required to be sent under this Act or is in default of paying a required fee.

Section.Text: Sections 265 and 266 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If there is an error in articles, a notice, a certificate or other document, the directors or shareholders of the corporation shall, on the request of the Director, pass the resolutions and send to the Director the documents required to comply with this Act, and take such other steps as the Director may reasonably require so that the Director may correct the document.
Before proceeding under subsection (1), the Director must be satisfied that the correction would not prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of the corporation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corrections at the request of the corporation
Subsection.Text: The Director may, at the request of the corporation or of any other interested person, accept a correction to any of the documents referred to in subsection (1) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the correction is approved by the directors of the corporation, unless the error is obvious or was made by the Director; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Director is satisfied that the correction would not prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of the corporation and that the correction reflects the original intention of the corporation or the incorporators, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If, in the view of the Director, of the corporation or of any interested person who wishes a correction, a correction to any of the documents referred to in subsection (1) would prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of a corporation, the Director, the corporation or the person, as the case may be, may apply to the court for an order that the document be corrected and for an order determining the rights of the shareholders or creditors.
An applicant under subsection (4) shall give the Director notice of the application, and the Director is entitled to appear and to be heard in person or by counsel.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Director may require surrender of document
Subsection.Text: The Director may demand the surrender of the original document, and may issue a corrected certificate or file the corrected articles, notice or other document.
A corrected document shall bear the date of the document it replaces unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the correction is made with respect to the date of the document, in which case the document shall bear the corrected date; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the court decides otherwise.

Subsection.Text: If a corrected certificate materially amends the terms of the original certificate, the Director shall without delay give notice of the correction in a publication generally available to the public.
In the prescribed circumstances, the Director may cancel the articles and related certificate of a corporation.
Before proceeding under subsection (1), the Director must be satisfied that the cancellation would not prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of the corporation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Request to Director to cancel articles
Subsection.Text: In the prescribed circumstances, the Director may, at the request of a corporation or of any other interested person, cancel the articles and related certificate of the corporation if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the cancellation is approved by the directors of the corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Director is satisfied that the cancellation would not prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of the corporation and that the cancellation reflects the original intention of the corporation or the incorporators, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If, in the view of the Director, of the corporation or of any interested person who wishes a cancellation, a cancellation of articles and a related certificate would prejudice any of the shareholders or creditors of a corporation, the Director, the corporation or the person, as the case may be, may apply to the court for an order that the articles and certificate be cancelled and for an order determining the rights of the shareholders or creditors.
An applicant under subsection (4) shall give the Director notice of the application, and the Director is entitled to appear and to be heard in person or by counsel.
The Director may demand the surrender of a cancelled certificate.

A person who has paid the required fee is entitled during usual business hours to examine a document required by this Act or the regulations to be sent to the Director, except a report sent to the Director under subsection 230(2), and to make copies of or extracts from it.

The Director shall furnish any person with a copy, extract, certified copy or certified extract of a document required by this Act or the regulations to be sent to the Director, except a report sent under subsection 230(2).

Section.Text: Subsection 267(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Director is not required to produce any document, other than a certificate and attached articles or statement filed under section 262, after the expiration of the prescribed period.

Section.Text: Subsection 267.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Information or notices required by this Act to be summarized in a publication generally available to the public or published by the Director may be made available to the public or published by any system of mechanical or electronic data processing or by any other information storage device that is capable of reproducing any required information or notice in intelligible form within a reasonable time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 268(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may, by order, require that a body corporate incorporated by or under an Act of Parliament to which Part I or II of the Canada Corporations Act, chapter C-32 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1970, does not apply, apply for a certificate of continuance under section 187 within such period as may be prescribed except for the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a bank;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a company or society to which the Insurance Companies Act applies; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a company to which the Trust and Loan Companies Act applies.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 268(7) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A body corporate to which Part IV of the Canada Corporations Act, chapter C-32 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1970, applies, other than a body corporate that carries on a business referred to in paragraph (6)(b) or (c), may apply for a certificate of continuance under section 187.
Subsection 268(11) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
A body corporate that is incorporated by or under a Special Act, as defined in section 87 of the Canada Transportation Act, may apply for a certificate of continuance under section 187.

Section.Text: The French version of the Act is amended by replacing the word “appartenance” with the words “droit de propriété”, with the modifications that the circumstances require, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 6(1)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 173(1)(n);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 174(1)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 174(2), (3) and (4);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 174(6);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 176(1)(h);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 176(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 190(1)(a).

Section.Text: The English version of the Act is amended as set out in the schedule.

Section.MarginalNote: Review of Canada Business Corporations Act
Section.Text: A committee of the Senate, of the House of Commons or of both Houses of Parliament that is designated or established for the purpose shall, within five years after the coming into force of this section, and within every ten years thereafter, undertake a review of the provisions and operations of the Canada Business Corporations Act, and shall, within a reasonable period thereafter, cause to be laid before each House of Parliament a report thereon.

Subsection.Text: The definitions distributing cooperative, member loan, person and security in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Cooperatives Act are replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: coopérative ayant fait appel au public

Definition.Text: distributing cooperative means, subject to subsections 4(4) and (5), a distributing cooperative as defined in the regulations.

member loan means a loan required by the cooperative from its members as a condition of membership or to continue membership in the cooperative, and, for the purpose of Parts 8, 16, 17, 18.1 and 19 and subsection 163(2), a member loan is deemed to be a membership share issued at par value.
person means an individual or an entity, and includes a personal representative.

security includes an investment share, a debt obligation of a cooperative and a certificate evidencing such a share or debt obligation and, for the purposes of section 173 and Parts 18.1 and 19, includes a membership share.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: personal representative means a person who stands in place of and represents another person including, but not limited to, a trustee, an executor, an administrator, a receiver, an agent, a liquidator of a succession, a guardian, a tutor, a curator, a mandatary or an attorney.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Act, the word minor has the same meaning as in the applicable provincial law and, in the absence of any such law, has the same meaning as the word child in the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child, adopted in the United Nations General Assembly on November 20, 1989.

Section.Text: Subsections 4(4) to (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On the application of a cooperative, the Director may determine that the cooperative is not or was not a distributing cooperative if the Director is satisfied that the determination would not be prejudicial to the public interest.

The Director may determine that a class of cooperatives are not or were not distributing cooperatives if the Director is satisfied that the determination would not be prejudicial to the public interest.

Section.Text: Subsection 8(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application for incorporation of a cooperative may be made by a minimum of three persons, or by one or more cooperative entities, who intend to be members of the cooperative.

Section.Text: Subsection 12(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a notice that is required to be given under subsection 30(2) or 81(1) indicates that the cooperative, if it came into existence, would be in compliance with the Act.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 15(2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the designation of the classes or regional groups of members, if any, who may be represented by delegates,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the procedure for altering classes or regional groups of members, if applicable, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 15(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the division of members into classes or regional groups and, if so,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the qualifications for membership in each class or regional group,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the conditions precedent to membership in each class or regional group,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the method, time and manner of withdrawing from a class or regional group or transferring membership from one class or regional group to another and any applicable conditions on a transfer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the conditions on which membership in a class or regional group ends;

Section.Text: Paragraph 16(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contained undertakings by every member and the successors, assigns and personal representatives of every member to observe all the provisions of the articles and by-laws.

Section.Text: Paragraph 19(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make an order respecting the nature and extent of the obligations and liability under the contract of the cooperative and the person who entered into or purported to enter into the contract by or on behalf of the cooperative; and

Section.Text: Subsections 20(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The name of a cooperative may be set out in its articles in an English form, a French form, an English form and a French form, or a combined English and French form, so long as the combined form meets the prescribed criteria. The cooperative may use and be legally designated by any such form.
Subsection 28(1) of the Act is amended by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (e) and by replacing paragraphs (f) and (g) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a sale, lease or exchange of all or substantially all of the property of the cooperative was not authorized.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 28(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a person who has, or ought to have, knowledge of a situation described in that subsection by virtue of their relationship to the cooperative.

Section.Text: Subsection 31(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When records or registers kept outside Canada
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), but subject to the Income Tax Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Customs Act and any other Act administered by the Minister of National Revenue, a cooperative may keep all or any of its records mentioned in paragraphs (1)(a), (b), (c), (f) and (g) and (2)(a) and (b) at a place outside Canada, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the records are available for inspection, by means of a computer terminal or other technology, during regular office hours at the registered office or another office in Canada designated by the directors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the cooperative provides the technical assistance to facilitate an inspection referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: Subsection 32(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Inspection and copying of records by members, creditors and shareholders
Subsection.Text: Members, creditors and shareholders of the cooperative, their personal representatives and the Director may examine the records referred to in paragraphs 31(1)(a), (b), (c) (f) and (g) during the usual business hours of the cooperative and may take extracts from the records, free of charge, or have copies of them made after payment of a reasonable fee.

Section.Text: Subsection 33(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Members, shareholders and creditors of a cooperative and their personal representatives and, where the cooperative is a distributing cooperative, any other person, may request that the cooperative provide them with a list of members or shareholders, no later than ten days after the cooperative receives the affidavit referred to in subsection (2) and after payment of a reasonable fee.

Section.Text: Subsection 48(3) of Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Participation in meeting by electronic means
Subsection.Text: Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, a member or shareholder may participate in a meeting of the cooperative, in accordance with the regulations, if any, by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting, if the cooperative makes available such a communication facility.

If the directors of a cooperative, or any other person, call a meeting of the cooperative pursuant to this Act, those directors or that person, as the case may be, may determine that the meeting shall be held, in accordance with the regulations, if any, entirely by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting, if the by-laws so provide.

Section.Text: Subsection 50(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Order to delay calling of annual meeting
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the cooperative may apply to the court for an order extending the time for calling an annual meeting.
Subsection 51(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The directors may, within the prescribed period, fix in advance a date as the record date for the determination of the members or shareholders who are entitled to receive payment of a dividend or for any other purpose except the right to receive notice of, or to vote at, a meeting.
Subsections 51(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Record date — notice of shareholders’ meetings
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of determining the shareholders who are entitled to receive notice of a meeting of the shareholders, the directors may, within the prescribed period, fix in advance a date as the record date for that determination.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Record date — voting at shareholders’ meetings
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining the shareholders who are entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders, the directors may, within the prescribed period, fix in advance a date as the record date for that determination.
The portion of subsection 51(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a record date with respect to shareholders is fixed under this section, unless notice of the date is waived by each shareholder whose name is set out in the securities register at the close of business on the day the directors fix the record date, notice of the record date must be given within the prescribed period

Section.Text: Subsection 52(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Notice of the time and place of a meeting of a cooperative must be sent within the prescribed period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to each person who is entitled to vote at the meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to each director; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to the auditor of the cooperative, if any.

Subsection.Text: In the case of a cooperative that is not a distributing cooperative, the notice may be sent within a shorter period if so specified in the articles or the by-laws.
Subsections 58(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proposals by members or directors to amend articles
Subsection.Text: Any member or director may, in accordance with section 290, make a proposal to amend the articles.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proposals by other persons to amend articles
Subsection.Text: Any other person may, in accordance with section 290, make a proposal to amend the articles if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has been, for at least the prescribed period, the registered holder or the beneficial owner of at least the prescribed number of outstanding investment shares of the cooperative; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has the support of persons who, in the aggregate, and including or not including the person that submits the proposal, have been, for at least the prescribed period, the registered holders, or the beneficial owners of, at least the prescribed number of outstanding investment shares of the cooperative.

Subsection.Text: A proposal submitted by a person described in paragraph (2.1)(a) must be accompanied by the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of the person and of the person’s supporters, if applicable; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of investment shares held or owned by the person and by the person’s supporters, if applicable, and the date the investment shares were acquired.

Subsection.Text: The information provided under subsection (2.2) does not form part of the proposal or of the supporting statement referred to in subsection (3) and is not included for the purposes of the prescribed maximum word limit set out in subsection (3).

If requested by the cooperative within the prescribed period, a person who submits a proposal must provide proof, within the prescribed period, that the person meets the requirements of subsection (2.1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proposal and statement to accompany notice of meeting
Subsection.Text: A proposal submitted for consideration at a meeting must be attached to the notice of the meeting, together with, if requested by the person making the proposal, a statement in support of the proposal and the name and address of person making the proposal. The statement and the proposal must together not exceed the prescribed maximum number of words.
Paragraph 58(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proposal is not submitted to the cooperative at least the prescribed number of days before the anniversary date of the notice of meeting that was sent to members and shareholders in connection with the previous annual meeting;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(4)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it clearly appears that the primary purpose of the proposal is to enforce a personal claim or redress a personal grievance against the cooperative or its directors, officers, members or security holders;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 58(4)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: not more than the prescribed period before the receipt of a proposal, a person failed to present, at a meeting, a proposal that, at the person’s request, had been attached by the cooperative to the notice of the meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: substantially the same proposal was attached to a notice of meeting relating to a meeting of the cooperative held not more than the prescribed period before the receipt of the proposal and the proposal did not receive the prescribed minimum amount of support at the meeting; or

Subsection.Text: Section 58 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Cooperative may refuse to include proposal
Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person described in subsection (2.1) makes a proposal and fails to continue to hold or own the number of investment shares referred to in that subsection up to and including the day of the meeting, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member makes a proposal and, prior to the meeting, withdraws from membership in accordance with section 39,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the cooperative is not required to include in the notice of a meeting, or attach to it, any proposal submitted by that person for any meeting held within the prescribed period following the date of the meeting.

Section.Text: Subsections 60(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a cooperative refuses to include a proposal in a notice of a meeting referred to in section 52, the cooperative must, within the prescribed period after the day on which it receives the proposal or the day on which it receives the proof of ownership under subsection 58(2.4), as the case may be, notify in writing the person submitting the proposal of its intention to omit the proposal from the notice and of the reasons for the refusal.

On the application of a person submitting a proposal who claims to be aggrieved by a cooperative’s refusal under subsection (1), a court may restrain the holding of the meeting at which the proposal is sought to be presented and make any further order it thinks fit.

Section.Text: Section 65 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any vote referred to in subsection (1) may be held, in accordance with the regulations, if any, entirely by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility, if the cooperative makes available such a communication facility.

Unless the by-laws otherwise provide, a member or shareholder participating in a meeting of the cooperative under subsection 48(3) or (3.1) and entitled to vote at that meeting may vote, in accordance with the regulations, if any, by means of the telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that the cooperative has made available for that purpose.

Section.Text: Section 67 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting to the effect that the chairperson of the meeting declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the fact without proof of the number or proportion of the votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.
Section 70 of the Act is repealed.
Paragraphs 71(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is not feasible to call the meeting within the time or in the manner in which those meetings are to be called;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not feasible to conduct the meeting in the manner required by this Act or the by-laws; or

Section.Text: Subsection 78(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: At least twenty-five per cent of the directors must be resident in Canada. However, if the cooperative has only three directors, at least one director must be resident in Canada.

Section.Text: Subsections 83(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An individual who is elected or appointed to hold office as a director is not a director and is deemed not to have been elected or appointed to hold office as a director unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: he or she was present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place and he or she did not refuse to hold office as a director; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: he or she was not present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: he or she consented to hold office as a director in writing before the election or appointment or within ten days after it, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: he or she has acted as a director pursuant to the election or appointment.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 85(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (3), if there is a vacancy on the board of directors, except a vacancy because of an increase in the number or the minimum or maximum number of directors provided for in the articles or because of a failure to elect or appoint the number or minimum number of directors provided for in the articles, and there is still a quorum on the board, the remaining directors may
Subsection 85(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If all of the directors have resigned or been removed without replacement, a person who manages or supervises the management of the business and affairs of the cooperative is deemed to be a director for the purposes of this Act.

Section.Text: Section 91 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notice of change of director or directors address

Subsection.Text: A cooperative must, within fifteen days after

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a change is made among its directors, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it receives a notice of change of address of a director referred to in subsection (2),

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: send to the Director a notice, in the form that the Director fixes, setting out the change.

Subsection.Text: A director must, within fifteen days after changing his or her address, send the cooperative a notice of that change.
Any interested person, or the Director, may apply to a court for an order to require a cooperative to comply with subsection (1), and the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.
Subsection 97(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
To constitute a quorum,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at least twenty-five per cent of the directors at the meeting must be resident in Canada or, if the cooperative has only three directors, at least one of the directors at the meeting must be resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a majority of the directors at the meeting must be members of the cooperative, or representatives of members that are entities or members of members that are cooperative entities.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 97(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Despite subsection (1), a meeting of directors may be held without the number of directors resident in Canada required under that subsection if
Paragraph 97(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the required number would have been present had that director been present at the meeting.

Section.Text: Subsection 98(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the by-laws, a director may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, participate in a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting.

Section.Text: Subsection 100(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting to the effect that the chairperson of the meeting declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the fact without proof of the number or proportion of the votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.

Section.Text: Paragraph 101(3)(d) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 102(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 102(7) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

L’administrateur qui acquitte les dettes visées au présent article, dont l’existence est établie au cours d’une procédure soit de liquidation et de dissolution, soit de faillitte, a droit à toute priorité qu’aurait pu faire valoir l’employé et, le cas échéant, est subrogé aux droits constatés dans le jugement.

Section.Text: Subsection 103(7) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 104(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director who is interested in a contract or transaction referred to in subsection 103(1) may not vote on any resolution to approve the contract or transaction.

Section.Text: Sections 106 and 107 of the Act are replaced by the following:

A contract or transaction for which disclosure is required under section 103 is not invalid, and the director or officer is not accountable to the cooperative, its members or its shareholders for any profit realized from the contract or transaction, because of the director’s or officer’s interest in the contract or transaction or because the director was present or was counted to determine whether a quorum existed at the meeting of directors or committee of directors that considered the contract or transaction, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: disclosure of the interest was made in accordance with sections 103 to 105;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the directors approved the contract or transaction; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the cooperative when it was approved.

Section.Text: Even if the conditions of section 106 are not met, a director or officer, acting honestly and in good faith, is not accountable to the cooperative, its members or its shareholders for any profit realized from a contract or transaction for which disclosure is required under section 103 and the contract or transaction is not invalid by reason only of the interest of the director or officer in the contract or transaction if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction is approved or confirmed by special resolution at a meeting of the members;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disclosure of the interest was made to the members in a manner sufficient to indicate its nature before the contract or transaction was approved or confirmed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the cooperative when it was approved or confirmed.

Section.Text: If a director or officer of a cooperative fails to disclose an interest in a material contract or transaction in accordance with section 103, or otherwise fails to comply with sections 103 to 106.1, a court may, on the application of the cooperative or a member or shareholder, set aside the contract or transaction on any terms that it thinks fit or order that the director or officer account to the cooperative, its members or its shareholders for any profit realized from the contract or transaction.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 109(3)(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: pay a commission referred to in section 128, except as authorized by the directors;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 109(3) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (h), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (i) and by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: issue investment shares of a series under section 126 except as authorized by the directors.

Section.Text: Section 111 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A director is not liable under this Part if the director exercised the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would have exercised in comparable circumstances, including reliance in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the cooperative represented to the director by an officer of the cooperative or in a written report of the auditor of the cooperative fairly to reflect the financial condition of the cooperative; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by the professional person.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 113(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A cooperative may advance moneys to a director, officer or other individual for the costs, charges and expenses of a proceeding referred to in subsection (1). The individual must repay the moneys if the individual did not fulfil the conditions of subsection (3), unless the members and shareholders decide, by separate resolutions, that the individual need not repay the moneys.
Paragraph 113(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was not judged by the court or other competent authority to have committed any fault or omitted to do anything that the individual ought to have done; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 115(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A provision in the articles of the cooperative or in a unanimous agreement that restricts, in whole or in part, the powers of the directors to manage, or supervise the management of, the business and affairs of the cooperative or vests, in whole or in part, but only in members and subject to subsection 76(1), those powers, is valid.
Subsections 115(3) to (8) of the Act are replaced by the following:
A purchaser or transferee of an investment share that is subject to a unanimous agreement is deemed to be party to the unanimous agreement.

If notice is not given to the purchaser or transferee of the existence of a unanimous agreement, in the manner referred to in subsection 183(2) or otherwise, the purchaser or transferee may, no later than thirty days after they become aware of its existence, rescind the transaction by which they acquired the investment shares.

To the extent that a provision in the articles of the cooperative, or in a unanimous agreement, restricts the powers of the directors to manage, or supervise the management of, the business and affairs of the cooperative, members who are given that power to manage or supervise the management of the business and affairs of the cooperative have all the rights, powers, duties and liabilities of directors, whether they arise under this Act or otherwise, including any defences available to the directors, and the directors are relieved of their rights, powers, duties and liabilities, including their liabilities under section 102, to the same extent.
Nothing in this section prevents members from fettering their discretion when exercising the powers of directors under a unanimous agreement.

Section.Text: Subsection 123(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Charge on membership shares for amounts

Subsection.Text: A cooperative has a charge on a membership share or any amount standing to the credit of a member or the personal representative of a member for a debt of that member to the cooperative.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 126(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The articles may authorize, subject to any limitations set out in them and subject to subsection (2), the issue of any class of investment shares in one or more series and may do either or both of the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 129(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection 183(2), the articles may provide that the cooperative has a charge on an investment share registered in the name of a shareholder or the personal representative of a shareholder for a debt of the shareholder to the cooperative, including an amount unpaid as of the date a body corporate was continued under this Act, in respect of an investment share issued by it.
The portion of subsection 130(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection 290(3), a distributing cooperative that has issued investment shares that remain outstanding and are held by more than one person may, by special resolution of the members and by a separate special resolution of the shareholders of each class, amend its articles to constrain
Paragraph 130(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the issue, transfer or ownership of any investment share to assist the cooperative to comply with any prescribed law; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 131(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: complying with any prescribed law.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 131(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Les administrateurs doivent choisir les parts de placement à vendre en vertu du paragraphe (1) de bonne foi et de manière à ne pas se montrer injuste à l’égard des autres détenteurs de parts de placement de la catégorie soit en leur portant préjudice soit en ne tenant pas compte de leurs intérêts.
The portion of section 137 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Unless the articles, the by-laws or a unanimous agreement provide otherwise, the directors may, subject to subsection 126(2), without the authorization of the members or shareholders,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 137(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: give guarantees;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 138(3)(a)(i) and (ii) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: de biens d’une personne avec laquelle elle avait, au moment de l’échange, un lien de dépendance au sens de la Loi de l’impôt sur le revenu,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: d’actions ou de parts de placement d’une entité ou de droits ou d’intérêts dans celle-ci, lorsque la coopérative avait avec celle-ci, soit au moment de l’échange, soit en raison de celui-ci, un tel lien;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 138(3)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who, immediately before the exchange, dealt with the cooperative at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act, if the person, the cooperative and all the holders of shares in the class or series of shares so issued consent to the exchange; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 138(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For greater certainty, if a cooperative issues membership shares with a par value, the cooperative is deemed, for the purposes of subsection 147(2), sections 151 and 154 and paragraph 299(2)(d), to have a stated capital account for its membership shares that includes each amount that has been received by the cooperative for the membership shares.

Section.Text: Subsection 139(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 147(2), sections 151 and 154 and paragraph 299(2)(d), when a cooperative is continued under this Act, its stated capital account is deemed to include the amount that would have been included if the cooperative had been incorporated under this Act.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 147(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La coopérative ne peut faire aucun paiement en vue d’acquérir des parts de placement s’il existe des motifs raisonnables de croire que :

Section.Text: Section 160 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
The definitions intermediary and solicit or solicitation ” in subsection 163(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: intermediary means a person who holds a security on behalf of another person who is not the registered holder of the security, and includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a securities broker or dealer required to be registered to trade or deal in securities under the laws of any jurisdiction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a securities depositary;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a clearing agency, a securities dealer, trust company, bank or other person, including another clearing agency, on whose behalf the clearing agency or its nominees hold securities of an issuer;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee or administrator of a self-administered retirement savings plan, retirement income fund, education savings plan or other similar self-administered savings or investment plan registered under the Income Tax Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a nominee of a person referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a person who carries out functions similar to those carried out by individuals or entities referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) and that holds a security registered in its name, or in the name of its nominee, on behalf of another person who is not the registered holder of the security.

Definition.Text: solicit or solicitation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a request for a proxy whether or not accompanied by or included in a form of proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a request to execute or not to execute a form of proxy or to revoke a proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy or other communication to a shareholder under circumstances reasonably calculated to result in the procurement, withholding or revocation of a proxy, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy to a shareholder under section 165; but

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: does not include

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy in response to an unsolicited request made by or on behalf of a shareholder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the performance of administrative acts or professional services on behalf of a person soliciting a proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending by an intermediary of the documents referred to in section 169,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a solicitation by a person in respect of investment shares of which they are the beneficial owner,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a public announcement, as prescribed, by a shareholder of how the shareholder intends to vote and the reasons for that decision,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a communication for the purposes of obtaining the number of investment shares required for a proposal under subsection 58(2.1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a communication, other than a solicitation by or on behalf of the management of the cooperative, that is made to shareholders, in any circumstances that may be prescribed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 164(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For a proxy to be valid, it must be executed by the shareholder or by their personal representative authorized in writing.
The portion of paragraph 164(4)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by depositing a document in writing executed by the shareholder or by their personal representative authorized in writing

Section.Text: Paragraph 165(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it has fifty or fewer shareholders entitled to vote at a meeting, two or more joint holders being counted as one shareholder.

Section.Text: Subsection 166(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Solicitation to fifteen or fewer shareholders
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the cooperative, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular, if the total number of shareholders whose proxies are solicited is fifteen or fewer, two or more joint holders being counted as one shareholder.

Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the cooperative, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular, if the solicitation is, in the prescribed circumstances, conveyed by public broadcast, speech or publication.

Section.Text: Subsection 167(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On the application of any interested person, the Director may exempt, on any terms that the Director thinks fit, the person from any of the requirements of section 165 or subsection 166(1), which exemption may have retrospective effect.
Subsection 169(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An intermediary, or a proxyholder appointed by an intermediary, may not vote shares that the intermediary does not beneficially own and that are registered in the name of the intermediary or in the name of a nominee of the intermediary unless the intermediary or proxyholder, as the case may be, receives written voting instructions from the beneficial owner.
Subsection 169(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If a beneficial owner so requests and provides an intermediary with appropriate documentation, the intermediary must appoint the beneficial owner or a nominee of the beneficial owner as proxyholder.
The definitions business combination, insider and officer in subsection 171(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: business combination means an acquisition of all or substantially all the property of one entity by another, or an amalgamation of two or more entities, or any similar reorganization between or among two or more entities.
insider means, except in section 173,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of a distributing cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of a subsidiary of a distributing cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of an entity that enters into a business combination with a distributing cooperative; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed or retained by a distributing cooperative.

Definition.Text: officer means the chairperson of the board of directors, president, vice-president, secretary, treasurer, comptroller, general counsel, general manager, managing director, or any other individual who performs functions for an entity similar to those normally performed by an individual occupying any of those offices.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 171(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director or an officer of an entity that beneficially owns, directly or indirectly, shares of a distributing cooperative, or that exercises control or direction over shares of the distributing cooperative, or that has a combination of any such ownership, control and direction, carrying more than the prescribed percentage of voting rights attached to all of the outstanding shares of the distributing cooperative not including shares held by the entity as underwriter while those shares are in the course of a distribution to the public is deemed to be an insider of the distributing cooperative;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 171(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Pour l’application de la présente partie, la vente de parts de membre à des membres ou le versement d’un prêt de membre à une coopérative ne constitue pas un appel public à l’épargne.
Subsection 171(4) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 172 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No insider shall knowingly sell, directly or indirectly, a security of a distributing cooperative or any of its affiliates if the insider selling the security does not own or has not fully paid for the security to be sold.
No insider shall knowingly, directly or indirectly, buy a put or sell a call in respect of a security of the cooperative or any of its affiliates.

Despite subsection (1), an insider may sell a security that the insider does not own if the insider owns another security convertible into the security sold or an option or right to acquire the security sold and, no later than ten days after the sale, the insider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exercises the conversion privilege, option or right and delivers the security so acquired to the purchaser; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: transfers the convertible security, option or right to the purchaser.

Section.Text: Section 173 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, insider, with respect to a cooperative, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an affiliate of the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a director or an officer of the cooperative or of any persons described in paragraphs (b), (e) or (g);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a member who controls more than ten per cent of the voting rights that may be exercised to elect or appoint a director of the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a person who beneficially owns, directly or indirectly, shares of the cooperative or who exercises control or direction over shares of the cooperative, or a combination of any such ownership, control and direction, carrying more than the prescribed percentage of the voting rights attached to all of the outstanding shares of the cooperative other than shares held by the person as underwriter while those shares are in the course of a distribution to the public;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person, other than a person described in paragraph (g), employed or retained by the cooperative or by a person described in paragraph (g);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a person who engages in or proposes to engage in any business or professional activity with or on behalf of the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a person who received, while they were a person described in any of paragraphs (a) to (g) material confidential information concerning the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a person who receives material confidential information from a person described in this subsection or in subsection (2) or (2.1), including from a person described in this paragraph, and who knows or who ought reasonably to have known that the person giving the information is a person described in this subsection or in subsection (2) or (2.1), including a person described in this paragraph; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, a person who proposes to make a take-over bid (as defined in the regulations) for securities of a cooperative, or to enter into a business combination with a cooperative, is an insider of the cooperative with respect to material confidential information obtained from the cooperative and is an insider of the cooperative for the purposes of subsection (6).

An insider of a person referred to in subsection (2), and an affiliate or associate of such a person, is an insider of the cooperative referred to in that subsection. Paragraphs (1)(b) to (j) apply in determining whether a person is such an insider except that references to “cooperative” in those paragraphs are to be read as references to “person described in subsection (2)”.
For the purposes of this section, the following are deemed to be a security of the cooperative:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a put, call or option or other right or obligation to purchase or sell a security of the cooperative; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a security of another entity whose market price varies materially with the market price of the securities of the cooperative.

Subsection.Text: An insider who purchases or sells a security of the cooperative with knowledge of confidential information that, if generally known, might reasonably be expected to affect materially the value of any of the securities of the cooperative is liable to compensate the seller of the security or the purchaser of the security, as the case may be, for any damages suffered by the seller or purchaser as a result of the purchase or sale, unless the insider establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the insider reasonably believed that the information had been generally disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information was known, or ought reasonably to have been known, by the seller or purchaser; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase or sale of the security took place in the prescribed circumstances.

Subsection.Text: The insider is accountable to the cooperative for any benefit or advantage received or receivable by the insider as a result of a purchase or sale described in subsection (4) unless the insider establishes the circumstances in paragraph 4(a).

An insider who discloses to another person confidential information with respect to the cooperative that has not been generally disclosed and that, if generally known, might reasonably be expected to affect materially the value of any of the securities of the cooperative is liable to compensate for damages any person who subsequently sells securities of the cooperative to, or purchases securities of the cooperative from, any person that received the information, unless the insider establishes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the insider reasonably believed that the information had been generally disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the information was known, or ought reasonably to have been known, to the person who alleges to have suffered the damages;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the disclosure of the information was necessary in the course of the business of the insider, except if the insider is a person described in subsection (2) or (2.1); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the insider is a person described in subsection (2) or (2.1), that the disclosure of the information was necessary to effect the take-over bid or the business combination, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: The insider is accountable to the cooperative for any benefit or advantage received or receivable by the insider as a result of a disclosure of the information as described in subsection (6) unless the insider establishes the circumstances in paragraph (6)(a), (c) or (d).

The court may assess damages under subsection (4) or (6) in accordance with any measure of damages that it considers relevant in the circumstances. However, in assessing damages in a situation involving a security of a distributing cooperative, the court must consider the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the plaintiff is a purchaser, the price paid by the plaintiff for the security less the average market price of the security over the twenty trading days immediately following general disclosure of the information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the plaintiff is a seller, the average market price of the security over the twenty trading days immediately following general disclosure of the information, less the price that the plaintiff received for the security.

Subsection.Text: If more than one insider is liable under subsection (4) or (6) with respect to the same transaction or series of transactions, their liability is joint and several, or solidary.
An action to enforce a right created by subsections (4) to (7) may be commenced only within two years after discovery of the facts that gave rise to the cause of action.

Section.Text: The definitions offeree cooperative and take-over bid in section 174 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: offeree cooperative means a distributing cooperative whose shares are the object of a take-over bid.

take-over bid means an offer made by an offeror to shareholders of a distributing cooperative at approximately the same time to acquire all of the shares of a class of issued shares and includes an offer made by a distributing cooperative to repurchase all of the shares of a class of its shares.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 175(8) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A cooperative that is an offeror making a take-over bid to repurchase all of the shares of a class of its shares is deemed to hold in trust for the dissenting shareholders the amounts that it would have had to pay or transfer to a dissenting offeree if the dissenting offeree had elected to accept the take-over bid under subparagraph (4)(b)(i), and the cooperative must deposit the amounts in a separate account in a body corporate any of whose deposits are insured by the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation or guaranteed by the Quebec Deposit Insurance Board or by any other similar entity created by the law of another province, and must place any thing received in lieu of money in the custody of such a body corporate.
Subparagraph 175(9)(c)(ii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the offeree cooperative or some designated person holds in trust for the dissenting shareholder the money or other things to which that shareholder is entitled as payment for or in exchange for the shares, and

Section.Text: Subsection 176(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a shareholder holding shares of a distributing cooperative does not receive a notice under this Part, the shareholder may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within ninety days after the date of the end of the take-over bid, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the shareholder did not receive an offer pursuant to the take-over bid, within ninety days after the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date of the end of the take-over bid, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the date on which the shareholder learned of the take-over bid,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: require the offeror to acquire those shares.

Section.Text: Subsection 182(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A security certificate must be signed by at least one of the following persons, or a facsimile of the signature must be reproduced on the certificate:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director, or an individual on their behalf, or an officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a transfer agent or branch transfer agent of the cooperative, or an individual on their behalf; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee who certifies it in accordance with a trust indenture.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 183(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

No restriction, charge, agreement or endorsement described in subsection (3) is effective against a transferee of a security, issued by a cooperative or by a body corporate before it is continued under this Act, who has no actual knowledge of the restriction, charge, agreement or endorsement unless it or a reference to it is noted conspicuously on the security certificate.
The restrictions, charges, agreements and endorsements referred to in subsection (2) are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a restriction on transfer other than a constraint under section 130;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a charge in favour of the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a unanimous agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an endorsement under subsection 302(10).

Subsection.Text: If the issued investment shares of a distributing cooperative remain outstanding and are held by more than one person, the cooperative must not restrict the transfer or ownership of its investment shares of any class or series except by way of a constraint under section 130.
Paragraph 183(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to comply with any prescribed law.

Section.Text: The heading before section 247 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 249(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The members and shareholders of a cooperative and their personal representatives may on request examine the statements referred to in subsection (1) during the usual business hours of the cooperative and may take extracts from them free of charge.

Section.Text: Subsection 252(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A distributing cooperative, any of the issued securities of which remain outstanding and are held by more than one person, must send a copy of the documents referred to in section 247 to the Director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not less than twenty-one days before each annual meeting of members or, without delay after a resolution referred to in paragraph 251(c) is signed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any event within fifteen months after the last preceding annual meeting of shareholders was held or the resolution referred to in paragraph 251(c) was signed, but no later than six months after the end of the cooperative’s preceding financial year.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 255(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A cooperative that is not a distributing cooperative may resolve not to appoint an auditor by

Section.Text: Subsection 260(5) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Lorsque la coopérative se propose de remplacer le vérificateur, pour cause de révocation ou d’expiration de son mandat, elle doit soumettre une déclaration motivée et le nouveau vérificateur a le droit de soumettre une déclaration commentant ces motifs.

Section.Text: Subsection 267(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La présente partie s’applique à tout acte de fiducie prévoyant une émission de titres de créance par voie d’appel public à l’épargne.

Section.Text: Paragraph 289(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: reduce or increase its stated capital, if its stated capital is set out in the articles;

Section.Text: Subsection 290(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), a person referred to in subsection 58(2) or (2.1) may make a proposal to amend the articles and section 58 applies, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to any meeting of the cooperative at which the proposal is to be considered.

Section.Text: Subsection 294(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors may at any time, and must when reasonably so directed by the Director, restate the articles of incorporation.
Subparagraph 298(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: except as may be prescribed, the articles of amalgamation be the same as the articles of the amalgamating holding cooperative, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 298(2)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: except as may be prescribed, the articles of amalgamation be the same as the articles of the amalgamating subsidiary cooperative whose shares are not cancelled, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 307(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

This Part, other than sections 308 and 311, does not apply to a cooperative that is an insolvent person or a bankrupt as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act.
Subsection 307(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Any proceedings taken under this Part to dissolve or to liquidate and dissolve a cooperative are stayed if the cooperative is at any time found, in a proceeding under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, to be an insolvent person as defined in that Act.
The portion of subsection 308(6) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

In the same manner and to the same extent as if it had not been dissolved, but subject to any reasonable terms that may be imposed by the Director, to the rights acquired by any person after its dissolution and to any changes to the internal affairs of the cooperative after its dissolution, the revived cooperative is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: restored to its previous position in law, including the restoration of any rights and privileges whether arising before its dissolution or after its dissolution and before its revival; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 308(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Any legal action respecting the affairs of a revived cooperative taken between the time of its dissolution and its revival is valid and effective.
In this section, interested person includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a member, a shareholder, a director, an officer, an employee and a creditor of the dissolved cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person who has a contractual relationship with the dissolved cooperative; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee in bankruptcy for the dissolved cooperative.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 311(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: does not have any directors or is in the situation described in subsection 85(6).

Subsection.Text: Section 311 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Despite anything in this section, the Director may dissolve a cooperative by issuing a certificate of dissolution if the fee for the issuance of a certificate of incorporation is not paid.

Section.Text: Paragraph 312(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: failed for two or more consecutive years to comply with the requirements of this Act with respect to the holding of annual meetings;

Section.Text: The portion of paragraph 313(1)(b) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: il constate qu’elle abuse des droits des membres, détenteurs de parts de placement, détenteurs de valeurs mobilières, créanciers, administrateurs ou dirigeants, ou se montre injuste à leur égard en leur portant préjudice ou en ne tenant pas compte de leurs intérêts :

Section.Text: Subsection 321(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A liquidator is not liable under this Part if the liquidator exercised the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would have exercised in comparable circumstances, including reliance in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the cooperative represented to the liquidator by an officer of the cooperative or in a written report of the auditor of the cooperative fairly to reflect the financial condition of the cooperative; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by the professional person.

Section.Text: Subsection 326(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, member and shareholder include their heirs and personal representatives.

Section.Text: Subsection 328(3) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 329(2)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: que la coopérative ou toute autre personne morale de son groupe, soit par la façon dont elle conduit ou a conduit ses affaires tant commerciales qu’internes, soit par la façon dont ses administrateurs exercent ou ont exercé leurs pouvoirs, abuse des droits des membres ou des détenteurs de valeurs mobilières ou se montre injuste à leur égard soit en leur portant préjudice soit en ne tenant pas compte de leurs intérêts;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 337:
The definitions in this section apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: financial interest, with respect to a cooperative, includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a title to or an interest in capital, assets, property, profits, earnings or royalties;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an option or other interest in, or a subscription to, a security;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an agreement under which the interest of the purchaser is valued for purposes of conversion or surrender by reference to the value of a proportionate interest in a specified portfolio of assets;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an agreement providing that money received will be repaid or treated as a subscription for shares, units or interests at the option of any person or the cooperative;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a profit-sharing agreement or certificate;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a lease, claim or royalty in oil, natural gas or mining, or an interest in the lease, claim or royalty;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: an income or annuity contract that is not issued by an insurance company governed by an Act of Parliament or a law of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: an investment contract; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: anything that is prescribed to be a financial interest.

Definition.Text: financial loss means a financial loss arising out of an error, omission or misstatement in financial information concerning a cooperative that is required under this Act or the regulations.
third party includes any subsequent party that is joined in proceedings before a court.

Subsection.Text: This Part applies to the apportionment of damages awarded to a plaintiff for financial loss after a court has found more than one defendant or third party responsible for the financial loss.
This Part does not apply to an award of damages to any of the following plaintiffs:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or a federal or provincial Crown corporation or government agency, unless a substantial part of its activities involves trading, including making investments in, securities or other financial instruments;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a charitable organization, private foundation or public foundation within the meaning of subsection 149.1(1) of the Income Tax Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an unsecured creditor in respect of goods or services that the creditor provided to a cooperative.

Subsection.Text: Subject to this section and sections 337.4 to 337.6, every defendant or third party who has been found responsible for a financial loss is liable to the plaintiff only for the portion of the damages that corresponds to their degree of responsibility for the loss.

If any part of the damages awarded against a responsible defendant or third party is uncollectable, the court may, on the application of the plaintiff, reallocate that amount to the other responsible defendants or third parties, if the application is made within one year after the date that the judgment was made enforceable.

The amount that may be reallocated to each of the other responsible defendants or third parties under subsection (2) is calculated by multiplying the uncollectable amount by the percentage that corresponds to the degree of responsibility of that defendant or third party for the total financial loss.

The maximum amount determined under subsection (3), in respect of any responsible defendant or third party, may not be more than fifty per cent of the amount originally awarded against that responsible defendant or third party.

The plaintiff may recover the whole amount of the damages awarded by the court from any defendant or third party who has been held responsible for a financial loss if it was established that the defendant or third party acted fraudulently or dishonestly.
The defendant or third party referred to in subsection (1) is entitled to claim contribution from any other defendant or third party who is held responsible for the loss.

Heading.TitleText: Joint and Several, or Solidary, Liability

Subsection.Text: Defendants and third parties referred to in subsection 337.2(1) are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for the damages awarded to a plaintiff who is an individual or a personal body corporate and who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: had a financial interest in a cooperative on the day that an error, omission or misstatement in financial information concerning the cooperative occurred, or acquired a financial interest in the period between the day that the error, omission or misstatement occurred and the day, as determined by the court, that it was generally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has established that the value of the plaintiff’s total financial interest in the cooperative was not more than the prescribed amount at the close of business on the day that the error, omission or misstatement occurred or at the close of business on any day that the plaintiff acquired a financial interest in the period referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), personal body corporate means a body corporate that is not actively engaged in any financial, commercial or industrial business and that is controlled by an individual or a group of individuals, each member of which is connected by blood relationship, adoption or marriage or by cohabiting with another member in a conjugal relationship.
Subsection (1) does not apply when the plaintiff brings the action as a member of a partnership or other association or as a trustee in bankruptcy, liquidator or receiver of a body corporate.

If the value of the plaintiff’s total financial interest referred to in subsection 337.5(1) is greater than the prescribed amount, a court may nevertheless determine that the defendants and third parties are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable if the court considers that it is just and reasonable to do so.
The Governor in Council may establish factors that the court shall take into account in deciding whether to hold the defendants and third parties jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to the factors referred to in subsection (2), but the factors shall be published in Part I of the Canada Gazette.

When, in order to establish the value of the total financial interest referred to in subsection 337.5(1), it is necessary to determine the value of a security that is traded on an organized market, the value of the security is, on the day specified in subsection (3),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the closing price of that class of security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if no closing price is given, the average of the highest and lowest prices of that class of security; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the security was not traded, the average of the bid and ask prices of that class of security.

Subsection.Text: The court may adjust the value of a security that has been determined under subsection (1) when the court considers it reasonable to do so.

The value of the security is to be determined as of the day that the error, omission or misstatement occurred. If the security was acquired in the period between that day and the day, as determined by the court, that the error, omission or misstatement was generally disclosed, the value is to be determined as of the day that it was acquired.

In this section, organized market means a recognized exchange for a class of securities or a market that regularly publishes the price of that class of securities in a publication that is generally available to the public.
The court shall determine the value of all or any part of a financial interest that is subject to resale restrictions or for which there is no organized market.
